nonBlockingIssues.description: .oauth-status-dot-missing CSS class has no explicit background color. The status dot is invisible for unauthenticated backends, though 'Not connected' text is shown alongside. A gray default color (e.g., var(--text-dim)) would improve visual consistency.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: All pages that embed HTMX-powered fragments must include the HTMX library (<script src="/ui/static/htmx.min.js"></script>) in their <head>. When adding new HTMX fragments, verify the parent page loads HTMX. Pages currently loading HTMX: dashboard.html, config.html, models.html, settings.html.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: oauth-web-ui feature embedded oauth_status.html (uses hx-get, hx-trigger, hx-swap) into settings.html, which originally didn't load HTMX. This broke the 30-second auto-refresh. Fixed in fix-oauth-ui-css-htmx.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: When adding CSS class names to HTML templates, ensure the corresponding CSS rules are added to the appropriate <style> block or external stylesheet. The project uses inline <style> blocks in templates (e.g., settings.html has ~460 lines of inline CSS). New classes must have corresponding rules using existing CSS custom properties for dark/light mode support.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: oauth-web-ui feature added ~25 CSS class names to oauth_status.html but never added CSS definitions. This is the second time CSS was missed — a pattern suggesting unclear convention. Fixed in fix-oauth-ui-css-htmx by adding ~160 lines of CSS.
